description_long: This church is a large, rectangular stone building with a prominent bell tower rising from one end, featuring vertical slits and a flat top. The main facade is adorned with a large circular rose window surrounded by ornate stonework, and the side walls are lined with tall arched windows and decorative buttresses. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in dark slate tiles, with several gabled sections and a polygonal apse at the rear. The exterior walls are constructed from a mix of light and dark stone, creating a striped effect, and the base is surrounded by a small row of trees and shrubbery. The entrance is marked by a set of double doors beneath the rose window, and the overall structure is symmetrical and imposing, typical of Romanesque Revival architecture. The church sits on a landscaped plot, with paved walkways and greenery enhancing its stately appearance.
description: A large stone church with a tall bell tower, rose window, arched windows, and a steep slate roof, surrounded by trees and shrubbery. The building features ornate stonework and a symmetrical, Romanesque Revival design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long nave, rose window, arched windows, and the bell tower on the left.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the bell tower with vertical slits, main entrance, and rows of trees.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the nave, rose window, and bell tower on the right.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the polygonal apse, arched windows, and part of the nave roof.
